summary.intent: Investigate GitHub issue #556 about vision support being silently broken for Mistral and Qwen models, and implement a fix if the bug was confirmed.
summary.outcome: Bug confirmed and fixed: `supports_vision_for_model()` was rewritten from a default-deny allowlist to a default-allow denylist approach, with all 12 related tests passing and clippy/format clean. Commit created.

repo: Vision support is gated by `supports_vision_for_model()` in `crates/providers/src/lib.rs` (lines 776-808), which was a prefix-based allowlist — Mistral and Qwen were missing.
When vision is unsupported, `tool_result_to_content()` in `runner.rs:728-732` calls `sanitize_tool_result()` which silently strips image data — no error is surfaced to the user.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker (separate from GitHub) that requires claiming issues before working on them.
GLM models use a naming convention where 'v' in the model ID suffix indicates vision capability (e.g., glm-4v vs glm-4).

code.finding: supports_vision_for_model() was a default-deny allowlist; rewritten to default-allow with a denylist of known text-only models (codestral, kimi-, non-v GLM variants).
tool_result_to_content() checks supports_vision and calls sanitize_tool_result() to strip images — this is the silent failure point when vision is incorrectly denied.

workflow: Default-deny capability flags are dangerous for silently degrading UX; default-allow with a known-bad denylist produces visible API errors that are easier to diagnose.
The 'beads' issue tracker can be flaky/rate-limited, requiring retry logic or waiting before closing issues.

friction: The beads issue tracker was unresponsive/down during the session — multiple retries to close the issue all failed, requiring the close to be deferred.
Multiple rounds of grepping for `supports_vision` were needed across different contexts before getting a complete picture of the call chain.
open_items: Close the beads issue once the tracker is back online.
Consider expanding the MISTRAL_MODELS list in lib.rs to surface more Mistral vision-capable models in the UI.
